<system_instruction>
You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/montevideo-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.
The target branch for this workspace is main. Use this for actions like creating new PRs, bisecting, etc., unless you're told otherwise.

If the user asks you to work on several unrelated tasks in parallel, you can suggest they start new workspaces.
If the user asks for help with Conductor, ask them to email [humans@conductor.build](mailto:humans@conductor.build) or
click on the comment icon in the bottom left to send feedback.
</system_instruction>

That completely makes sense—an environment where you set strategy, the graph acts as shared memory, and a central governance feed is where you manage your agents instead of fighting context loss across 15 tools.

Since you're building this for yourself first as the ideal customer, what are the first couple of specific agents you're trying to orchestrate on this platform right away?

task:
This is a very compelling and well-defined vision for an PROJECTagent-native business operating system. It eloquently captures the core pain point of fractured agent context and re-explanation, reframing it as a knowledge graph that is live, contextual, and reasoned over by all agents. The combination of real-time graph queries, relationship reasoning, authority scopes, continuous knowledge ingestion from meetings and conversations, conflict detection, and a unified governance feed creates a unique and powerful environment.

Your focus on solo founders and small teams as your initial target market is pragmatic and offers a clear go-to-market differentiation while building toward enterprise scalability.

To deepen my understanding and help you best: What current projects or milestones are you working on first to build out this agent-native OS? For example, are you prioritizing graph data ingestion pipelines, the agent interface and decision governance feed, or the identity and authority layer?

I am prioritizing the graph model itself

To advance building the graph model as the foundational element for your PROJECTagent-native business operating system, key next steps include:

Developing the core graph data structures to capture entities and relationships
Implementing relationship management and linking capabilities for data connectivity
Building a reasoning engine to enable inference and decision logic across the graph
Defining a robust data schema for extensibility and integrity
Implementing an efficient graph storage layer with fast retrieval and updates
Creating APIs for agents and system components to access and manipulate the graph
Integrating the graph model seamlessly with existing agent systems
Optimizing query performance for real-time responsiveness
Would you like me to help break these down into detailed actionable features and tasks you can track and prioritize?

yes

Here's a detailed breakdown of actionable features and tasks to prioritize for buildin

I can see you're in onboarding mode — wrapping up the vision for your agent-native business operating system. Let me check what's been captured so far and give you a summary.Perfect. Here's what I've captured from your onboarding conversation:

Project: Agent-Native Business Operating System

Core Positioning

Problem: Solo founders/tiny teams spend 30-40% of time re-explaining context across agent sessions. Current tools (Claude Code, etc.) start fresh each session, losing decisions and constraints.
Solution: Graph as shared memory instead of human brain. Agents query the graph for full context via MCP, make provisional decisions when needed, and surface conflicts automatically.
Target Market & GTM

Initial wedge: Solo founders and tiny teams already using AI agents but hitting coordination walls
Advantage: Single decision-maker (no org buy-in needed), immediate value for people already frustrated with context loss
Later: Enterprise play once graph proves out
Key Product Elements

Governance Feed: One place for pending agent decisions, cross-project conflicts, stale commitments, deferred questions
Cross-project intelligence: Automatic conflict detection ("this billing decision conflicts with your auth rate limiting")
Continuous extraction: Meeting transcripts, Slack threads, docs feed the graph in real-time
IAM & Authority: Agents have scoped authority (provisional vs confirmed decisions)
Full context injection: MCP + hooks give coding agents all decisions/constraints at session start
Visualization: Interactive relationship graph showing project structure, dependencies, conflicts
Current Status

Project entity created
No work items, decisions, or questions captured yet
Ready to start building
Does this capture the core vision? Anything you'd like to adjust or add before we start creating the first work items?
